question: How many named storms will the 2026 Eastern Pacific hurricane season have had through July 23, 2026?

factors.description: 4 storms already formed — hard floor; count cannot be below 4
NHC Area 1 has 90% 7-day genesis — very likely 5th storm (Elida) before July 23
NHC Area 5 has 60% 7-day genesis — decent chance of 6th storm
Below-normal ACE suggests weak/sheared systems that could stall as depressions short of TS strength
Active July basin; possible late-window third genesis

summary: The 2026 EPAC season has 4 named storms as of July 13 (Amanda, Boris, Cristina, Douglas), a hard floor. NHC's operational Tropical Weather Outlook flags two strong genesis candidates east of 140°W: Area 1 offshore SW Mexico (90% 7-day genesis → likely Elida, 5th storm) and Area 5 new low off southern Mexico (60% 7-day → potential 6th). A Monte Carlo built from these probabilities (0.88, 0.58, 0.10 for a third, plus Poisson(0.35) background genesis for late-window systems) yields P(4)=3%, P(5)=29%, P(6)=46%, P(7)=18%, P(8)=4%, P(9)=0.5% — mean 5.9, median 6, mode 6. Reaching 5 is near-certain given the 90% candidate; the 6th hinges on the 60% system.
reasoning:
My forecast for the number of named storms in the 2026 Eastern Pacific hurricane season through July 23, 2026:

**Median: 6 named storms** (mode 6, mean 5.9)

**Distribution:**
- 4 storms: ~3%
- 5 storms: ~29%
- 6 storms: ~46%
- 7 storms: ~18%
- 8 storms: ~4%
- 9+ storms: <1%

**Key reasoning:** The season has a hard floor of 4 (Amanda, Boris, Cristina, Douglas). As of July 13, NHC's Tropical Weather Outlook shows two strong genesis candidates east of 140°W — Area 1 offshore SW Mexico at 90% (likely to become Elida, the 5th storm) and a new low off southern Mexico at 60% (potential 6th). Reaching 5 is near-certain; the 6th depends on the second system organizing. I applied small haircuts to the NHC genesis odds since named-storm status requires reaching tropical-storm strength (39 mph), and this season's below-normal ACE hints at weaker systems that could stall as depressions.
